en.changeSummary: {ch1}Conditions have deteriorated significantly from what was previously forecast: the midday period is now seeing active thunderstorms with large hail and very strong, gusty winds — well beyond the plain rainy day that was committed. {ch2}This is a severe weather situation; the storms carry the potential for damaging winds and hail, so please take appropriate precautions through {q:time:2026-04-21T18:00:00Z}.
en.closing: Rain and overcast skies are expected to persist into the evening, though the severe threat should ease after the midday thunderstorm window. Keep an eye on local alerts and stay sheltered until the storms have passed.

input.reasoning_trace:
Step 1 — TAF vs. GFS: The TAF (issuance 2026-04-21T11:38Z) is newer than the GFS run (2026-04-21T06Z), so TAF prevails for blocks within its validity window. The TAF base covers the full remaining period with showers and overcast; the 15Z amendment refines to light showers/rain. No TAF group explicitly upgrades to severe.

Step 2 — Observation override: The METAR at 13:53Z (within/just before the 12–18Z block) reports active heavy thunderstorm + rain + HAIL, winds 230°/25 mph gusting 47 mph (~22 kt sustained, ~41 kt gust), cumulonimbus at 2500 ft. This observation falls at the leading edge of the 12–18Z block and overrides that block's content. The provisional and prior both had precipPhenomenon=rain, severeFlag=false for that block. The observation reveals: (a) phenomenon must be upgraded to thunderstorm; (b) hail and strong gusts indicate a severe storm — severeFlag→true; (c) winds upgraded from 7–12 kt to 18–25 kt range reflecting the observed 22 kt sustained / ~41 kt gust (capped conservatively since gusts are already partly passing); (d) precipExpectation upgraded to certain given ongoing activity. The 18Z block is left at likely/rain/no-severe per TAF and GFS (no observation support to change it, and the 30% prob thunderstorm group 19–23Z is only 30%, not enough to flip to certain).

Step 3 — Prior snapshot comparison: Prior had precipPhenomenon=rain, severeFlag=false for the 12–18Z block. Reconciled final has precipPhenomenon=thunderstorm, severeFlag=true for that block. This is a safety-critical change (severe thunderstorm with hail appearing where plain rain was committed). This is unambiguously news: weather arrived that was not promised and is hazardous. directional asymmetry fully applies. submit_reconciled_report is warranted.

Note on changed_since_last_sent_report="observation only": The anti-reversal rule prevents downgrading a severeFlag the model already set — here we are UPGRADING from false→true driven by a current observation of active severe weather (hail, high winds). This is the directional asymmetry case (hazardous weather arriving that was not promised), not a model-flip. The upgrade is justified.
